title: lora_l2.c + lichen_l2.c: lock ordering violation possible in enable path
description:
Cross-file interaction bug: Lock ordering documentation says tx_mutex before rx_mutex, but some paths may violate this.

lichen_l2.c:255-262 documents lock order: tx_mutex (1st) before rx_mutex (2nd).

Code paths that acquire both:
- lichen_l2_enable(true) lines 1059-1060: tx_mutex then rx_mutex CORRECT
- lichen_l2_enable(false) lines 1251-1252: tx_mutex then K_MSEC(100) rx_mutex CORRECT
- fail_late_init lines 1674-1675: tx_mutex then rx_mutex CORRECT
- re-init check lines 1547-1548: tx_mutex then rx_mutex CORRECT
- callback rollback lines 1118-1119: tx_mutex then rx_mutex CORRECT
- start failure rollback lines 1163-1164: tx_mutex then rx_mutex CORRECT

Now check lora_l2.c mutex patterns:
- lora_mutex and tx_buf_mutex are separate from lichen_l2's tx_mutex/rx_mutex

Cross-module call: lora_l2.c:959 calls lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() during deinit().
At that point, lora_l2.c:907-910 tried trylock on lora_mutex.
lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() reinits rx_mutex.

No cross-module lock ordering issue because:
- lora_l2.c uses lora_mutex + tx_buf_mutex
- lichen_l2.c uses tx_mutex + rx_mutex
- These are 4 different mutexes, no ordering between them documented

HOWEVER: lichen_l2_input() holds rx_mutex while calling lichen_link_rx() which... actually doesn't call back into lora_l2.c.

WITHDRAW: After thorough analysis, no lock ordering violation found between modules.
close_reason: not-a-bug: four separate mutexes with no cross-module locking
